title: Getting started with the Marine Sensitivity Atlas
text: single attach_atlas() call configures DuckDB connection S3 release creates table views, every reader score helper composes one connection. Use anon = TRUE anonymous public reads released tables.
code: con <- attach_atlas(anon = TRUE)  # the atlas tables are now queryable by name DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables                       WHERE table_schema = 'main' ORDER BY 1")$table_name #>  [1] "cell"         "cell_metric"  "dataset"      "metric"       "model"        #>  [6] "native_asset" "taxon"        "zone"         "zone_cell"    "zone_metric"

title: Getting started with the Marine Sensitivity Atlas
text: dataset STAC collection; model item whose assets point published Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs, PMTiles, Parquet. Read static catalog rstac::read_stac() (use rstac::stac() STAC API servers). Python reads catalog pystac:
code: library(rstac) v8 <- read_stac("https://file.marinesensitivity.org/stac/v8/collection.json")  # dataset collections under v8 basename(dirname(vapply(Filter(\(l) l$rel == "child", v8$links), \(l) l$href, character(1)))) #>  [1] "ms_merge"            "am"                  "ca_nmfs"             #>  [4] "ch_nmfs"             "ch_fws"              "rng_fws"             #>  [7] "bl"                  "rng_iucn"            "rng_turtle_swot_dps" #> [10] "gm"                  "nc" # one dataset's item + its published assets (native vs. gridded-model representations). # item links are relative to the collection, so resolve against its base URL. ch_base   <- "https://file.marinesensitivity.org/stac/v8/ch_fws/" ch        <- read_stac(paste0(ch_base, "collection.json")) item_href <- Filter(\(l) l$rel == "item", ch$links)[[1]]$href     # "./…json" it        <- read_stac(paste0(ch_base, sub("^\\./", "", item_href))) tibble(   asset = names(it$assets),   type  = vapply(it$assets, \(a) if (is.null(a$type)) "" else a$type, character(1)),   href  = vapply(it$assets, \(a) basename(a$href), character(1))) |>   knitr::kable() import pystac v8   = pystac.Collection.from_file("https://file.marinesensitivity.org/stac/v8/collection.json") item = next(pystac.Collection.from_file(     "https://file.marinesensitivity.org/stac/v8/ch_fws/collection.json").get_items()) {k: a.media_type for k, a in item.assets.items()}

title: Getting started with the Marine Sensitivity Atlas
text: native_asset table maps model published tile services. Retrieve merged taxon’s whole-range Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF hand cog_tile_url(), builds XYZ tile template drops straight leaflet map. (US-scored surface instead, cell_tile_url(mdl_key = mk) serves model_cell cells.)
code: library(leaflet) mk  <- "ms_merge|WORMS:137209"                    # Dermochelys coriacea (Leatherback Turtle)  # the published whole-range COG for this merged taxon cog <- tbl(con, "native_asset") |>   filter(ms_merge_key == mk, ds_key == "ms_merge") |>   pull(asset_url) |> head(1) url <- cog_tile_url(cog, colormap = "spectral_r", rescale = c(1, 100))  leaflet() |>   addProviderTiles("CartoDB.DarkMatter") |>   addTiles(urlTemplate = url, options = tileOptions(opacity = 0.9)) |>   setView(0, 20, zoom = 1)

title: Getting started with the Marine Sensitivity Atlas
text: scores_for_pra() returns per-category marine-sensitivity scores BOEM Program Area — ready flower plot, petal’s height category score.
code: sc <- scores_for_pra(con, "ALA")                  # ALA = a BOEM Program Area key knitr::kable(sc[c("component", "score")], digits = 1) plot_flower(   sc,   fld_category = component,   fld_height   = score,   fld_width    = even,   tooltip_expr = "{component}: {round(score, 1)}",   title        = "ALA")

title: Getting started with the Marine Sensitivity Atlas
text: taxon table carries merged taxon’s category, governing extinction-risk score, protection flags — e.g. highest-risk mammals US waters: go way — species occur set cells — use species_for_cells(con, cells), joins merged model_cell surface taxon (view needs credentials, call attach_atlas() without anon).
code: tbl(con, "taxon") |>   filter(sp_cat == "mammal", is_valid_usa, er_score >= 80) |>   select(scientific_name, common_name, extrisk_code, er_score, is_mmpa) |>   arrange(desc(er_score), scientific_name) |>   head(10) |>   collect() |>   knitr::kable()

title: Build an msens cell tile URL template — cell_tile_url
text: sql character(1); SELECT returning cell_id value columns colormap character; rio-tiler colormap name (default: "spectral_r") rescale numeric length-2 c(min, max) normalization; NULL lets server auto-compute SQL result tile request (prefer client-side value cell_stats() cache stability) color character(1); hex #rrggbb #rrggbbaa single-color mask. set, server renders every valid pixel flat color (ignoring colormap + rescale) — useful binary "cell present / cell outside X" overlays. mtime character; optional cache-bust tag, typically mtime source DuckDB file (e.g. file.info(sdm_db)$mtime). Distinct data version tag (v6, v7, ...) used paths. mdl_key character(1); STABLE model key fast-path. given (sql NULL), tile reads exactly one serving partition exact path — merged-model equivalent dense SQL point query, S3 LIST. titiler resolves mdl_key -> internal integer partition id model registry, URL keeps referencing model across releases even internal id renumbered. base character; base URL tile service. Defaults v8 titiler-v8 factory, accepts ?mdl_key=. (legacy titilecache Varnish caches v7 titiler, whose API takes ?sql= 422s mdl_key — must used v8 tiles.)

what: Rasterize range polygons onto the global 0.05° cell grid (whole range) — cells_from_ranges
title: Rasterize range polygons onto the global 0.05° cell grid (whole range) — cells_from_ranges
text: Turns presence polygons (IUCN/BOTW/NMFS range, critical-habitat polygon) (cell_id, value) v8 grid, capturing whole distribution — land ocean (v8 mask land). Uses exactextractr, fast robust messy geometry (self-intersections, MULTISURFACE — usual range-map "funk"). default every cell polygon overlaps gets value (100 = presence); cover = TRUE weights fraction cell covered. Marine share separate — see cells_pct_marine().

title: Rasterize range polygons onto the global 0.05° cell grid (whole range) — cells_from_ranges
code: cells_from_ranges(   x,   cellid_tif,   value = 100,   cover = FALSE,   min_coverage = 0,   max_vertices = 1000,   exact = FALSE )

title: Rasterize range polygons onto the global 0.05° cell grid (whole range) — cells_from_ranges
text: x sf/SpatVector (multi)polygons EPSG:4326 -180,180 cellid_tif path GLOBAL cell-id COG (cell_id every cell) value value covered cell. range datasets species' extinction-risk score compute_er_score() (e.g. compute_er_score("NMFS:EN") = 100, "NMFS:TN" = 50, "IUCN:CR" = 50) — NEVER hard-coded number. Default 100 convenience. cover logical; TRUE = coverage fraction × value, FALSE (default) = flat value overlap min_coverage keep cells whose covered fraction exceeds (default 0 = overlap; use 0.5 majority/centre-like rule) max_vertices (exact path ) subdivide range chunks many vertices rasterizing (default 1000). globe-spanning range (wide-ranging seabird) one massive multipolygon hangs st_union / exact_extract; subdividing bounds chunk's raster window never touches whole-globe polygon. Cells de-duplicated afterward, chunking equivalent union . Needs lwgeom package; without , falls back single-union path. exact force coverage-aware exact_extract path even flat -overlap case (default FALSE). default flat case (cover=FALSE, min_coverage=0) uses ~2x-faster terra::rasterize touches path, yields identical cell set; set TRUE boundary-exact coverage>0 behavior. cover=TRUE min_coverage>0 always use exact path.

Changing rule breaks documented case fails unit tests — guard merge logic silently lost wrong sequence minor tweak.

title: v8 per-taxon merge rules (single source of truth) — merge_sql
text: batch caller must first materialize three input relations connection: b (ms_merge_key, ds_key, cell_id, val) — raw model cells batch taxa (datasets; ds_key = '' AquaMaps, anything else range/expert dataset). taxon (ms_merge_key, er_score, ...) — governing extinction-risk score per taxon. taxon_flags (ms_merge_key, has_am, has_range) — GLOBAL presence flags crosswalk taxon_model (has_range = taxon non-dataset anywhere, just US). global scope enforces IUCN-range constraint — see . us_cells (cell_id) — -USA cell ids (scoring extent). merge_sql()$b_range, $b_am_all, $b_am_rng create intermediates; $global $us two output surfaces: GLOBAL viz surface ($global) = ∪ range (FULL OUTER range footprint valued governing er taxon's WHOLE footprint). -taxa omitted (reuse COGs). honest whole-range merged model painted COGs. US scoring surface ($us) = v7-faithful, US-boundary-aware, IUCN-CONSTRAINED: range footprint ∩ US valued max(er, --range) — BEYOND range MASKED (expert range constrains AquaMaps -prediction). Covers range-+ "" taxa. TRUE -taxa (GLOBAL has_range = FALSE, .e. range dataset anywhere) keep RAW ∩ US (dedup — taxon >1 AquaMaps model keeps duplicate cells). species expert range whose polygons lie ENTIRELY outside US therefore gets US presence: range ∩ US empty (1) excluded (2) global has_range TRUE. iucn_range_outside_us_eez exclusion (e.g. Sotalia guianensis, river dolphin AquaMaps -predicts US waters). Keying (2) GLOBAL has_range — range--US — crux; keying range--US silently re-introduces ~750 species.

title: v8 per-taxon merge rules (single source of truth) — merge_sql

title: msens 0.5.0
text: v8 “Marine Atlas” modeling + serving pass, part 2 — merge rules extracted package (single source truth) read/score API made compose one connection. Merge rules single source truth. New merge.R: merge_sql() turtle_sql() return exact SQL two-surface merge (global viz ∪ range; US-scoped, v7-faithful scoring surface AquaMaps -EEZ constraint) multiplicative turtle merge. workflows notebooks now call test-merge.R asserts (one synthetic fixture per taxon category), notebook tests can never drift. attach_atlas() now creates table views (via new exported atlas_views()), mirroring serving serve.duckdb, calc/score helpers reference bare table names (scores_for_pra(), species_for_cells(), scores_for_cells()) compose directly — e.g. scores_for_pra(attach_atlas(anon = TRUE), pra_key) just works. test-atlas.R guards . Getting-started article + STAC alignment. msens article now walks attach → browse → search STAC catalog → retrieve + map whole-range COG → score Program Area (flower plot) → species. v8 STAC catalog reads cleanly R (rstac::read_stac() static catalog — stac(), STAC API servers) Python (pystac); rstac added Suggests. cells_from_ranges() uses terra touches-rasterize fast default (keeping exact_extract coverage option) — big speed-large ranges. cell_tile_url() / cell_stats() default base → v8 titiler-v8 factory (accepts ?mdl_key=). legacy v7 titilecache Varnish takes ?sql= 422s mdl_key, left default-base callers (e.g. article’s map) blank tiles.

title: msens 0.4.0
text: v8 “Marine Atlas” foundation: read S3 Parquet release, ingest source models onto global 0.05° grid, publish native + gridded representations, emit STAC catalog. Read release. attach_atlas() — canonical DuckDB reader marine-atlas Parquet S3 (path-style, credential-chain), atlas_path() / atlas_tbl() accessors. Standardized Parquet + content-addressed change detection. write_atlas_parquet() / copy_atlas_parquet() (Parquet V2, zstd, ~80 MB byte-sized row groups) behind require_duckdb() version floor; hash_parquet() / hash_query() order-independent fingerprints + write_manifest() / force_target() deterministic, timestamp-free manifests; report_table() / report_parquet_summary() notebook ## Outputs sections. Stable model id. mdl_key_raw() / mdl_key_merged() build {ds_key}|{sp_id} key replaces volatile mdl_seq; renamed model-cell field value → val (SQL reserved word). Ingest helpers. cells_from_ranges() / cells_from_raster() / cells_pct_marine() rasterize source model onto global grid capturing whole range (land mask; pct_marine derived), via exactextractr. clean_sci_name() taxonomic matching. Native + gridded publishing. publish_cog() (COG overviews), publish_pmtiles() / publish_pmtiles_models() (per-model PMTiles), cog_tile_url() titiler /cog tiles. STAC v8 (stac.R): stac_build() collection/item generators emit native model representations per dataset model_cell Items, keyed stable mdl_key. Pipeline generator. build_targets_list() parses msens: front-matter workflow *.qmd targets list. pra_score_delta() version-equivalence gate. hex.R + interp.R marked DORMANT — v8 rolled back H3 grid 0.05° cell grid.
